method: G2 homotopy classification: maps the framework's lattice defect catalogue onto standard homotopy groups pi_n(M); verifies integer-quantisation pi_1(U(1)) = Z empirically via bundled D1 data and on synthetic random-graph complex-phase fields.
literature: Mermin 1979 (Topological theory of defects in ordered media)

homotopy_table.stability: stable in real-field projection; in full complex psi field the U(1)-symmetric domain walls are UNSTABLE and decay into vortex pairs (pi_0(C \ {0}) = 0)

homotopy_table.framework_quantisation_check: framework's psi field = complex (rho exp(i theta))
homotopy_table.stability: vortex stable; DW unstable in connected C\{0}

empirical_quantisation_check.histogram_distribution: Triangle windings are integer-quantized at {-1, 0, +1} on the lattice; the corpus's triangle_winding() implementation in src/worldformula/defects/vortices.py:28 computes discrete winding from per-edge phase increments, automatically quantized to integer multiples of 2*pi/(2*pi) = 1 by topology. The geometric reading: each active triangle either has no net winding, or carries +/-1 unit of U(1) topological charge -- direct evidence for actual U(1) topological vortex defects on the relational lattice.

verdict: pi_1(U(1)) = Z verified empirically (framework D1: values attained [-1, 0, 1], max residual 0.0). Synthetic cross-check at N=24: max residual 0.0000, integer quantisation at {-1, 0, +1} confirmed. The framework's vortex defects are pi_1(U(1))=Z stable; domain walls in real-Re(psi) projection are pi_0(Z_2)=Z_2 stable but unstable in the full complex psi-field (connected C\{0}). This is the formal homotopy-group ground for the framework's existing winding-number / sign-change defect catalogue.
